- **Stint 2, HARD, vueltas 14-36:** el primer stint con duro fue largo, de 23 vueltas, y tuvo una media de **88.22 s**, más rápida que la del stint inicial con SOFT. Además, la degradación fue **-0.060 s/vuelta**, es decir, los tiempos mejoraron según el dato provisto.

- **Stint 3, HARD, vueltas 37-53:** paró en la **vuelta 36 bajo SC/VSC** para montar otro HARD. Este stint fue de 17 vueltas, con media de **86.67 s** y degradación de **+0.063 s/vuelta**. Fue claramente más rápido en promedio que los dos stints anteriores.

- **Stint 4, SOFT, vueltas 54-58:** en la **vuelta 53**, también bajo **SC/VSC**, cambió de HARD a SOFT para las últimas 5 vueltas. Este último stint tuvo la mejor media de carrera para él: **86.62 s**, ligeramente más rápida que el stint anterior en HARD (**86.67 s**).

**Influencia en el resultado:** la estrategia le permitió terminar P1 combinando un tramo largo con HARD, dos paradas bajo **SC/VSC** según los datos, y un último stint corto con SOFT, que fue su stint de mejor promedio (**86.62 s**). Con los datos disponibles no se puede cuantificar cuánto tiempo ganó o perdió en cada parada, pero sí se observa que el cambio final a SOFT le dio sus vueltas promedio más rápidas justo al cierre de la carrera.

VER ran a 4-stint strategy on the 58-lap distance: soft (laps 1-13, 13 laps, avg 88.89 s), hard (laps 14-36, 23 laps, avg 88.22 s), hard (laps 37-53, 17 laps, avg 86.67 s), soft (laps 54-58, 5 laps, avg 86.62 s).  
Pits were at lap 13 (soft-hard), lap 36 (hard-hard under SC/VSC), and lap 53 (hard-soft under SC/VSC).  

Lap-time averages fell steadily across the four stints while the two SC/VSC stops permitted compound changes at minimal time cost, enabling completion of all 58 laps and the P1 result.

HAM used a one-stop strategy: medium tyres on laps 1-14 (avg 88.50 s, deg -0.047 s/lap), pitting on lap 14 to switch to hard tyres for laps 15-58 (44 laps, avg 87.74 s, deg -0.045 s/lap). This produced the P2 result.

SAI ran a one-stop strategy: SOFT tyres on laps 1-19 (19 laps at 90.07 s avg, -0.034 s/lap deg) then pitted on lap 19 for HARD tyres on laps 20-58 (39 laps at 88.64 s avg, -0.069 s/lap deg). The switch produced a lower average lap time across the longer second stint than the opening SOFT stint, directly yielding the P3 result.

TSU ran a three-stint strategy: 23 laps on medium (laps 1-23, avg 90.30 s, deg -0.068 s/lap), pitted lap 23 to hard for a 30-lap stint (laps 24-53, avg 88.44 s, deg -0.086 s/lap), then pitted lap 53 to soft under SC/VSC for the final 5 laps (avg 87.95 s).  

The hard stint delivered a lower average lap time than the preceding medium stint despite a steeper degradation rate. The SC/VSC-timed switch to soft on lap 53 enabled the quickest stint average (87.95 s) over the closing laps, producing the P4 result.

GAS ran a three-stint strategy on the 58-lap distance: HARD for laps 1-36 (avg 90.32 s, deg -0.088 s/lap), MEDIUM for laps 37-54 (avg 88.12 s, deg -0.156 s/lap), and SOFT for laps 55-58 (avg 88.34 s). Both stops occurred under SC/VSC (lap 36: HARD→MEDIUM; lap 54: MEDIUM→SOFT). The later stints posted lower average times than the opening HARD stint while the safety-car windows eliminated normal pit loss, enabling completion of the race for the P5 result.

BOT used a one-stop strategy: Medium tyres for laps 1-30 (30 laps, avg 90.07s, deg -0.079s/lap), then pitted on lap 30 to Hard tyres for laps 31-58 (28 laps, avg 88.17s, deg -0.001s/lap). The Hard stint produced a lower average time and near-zero degradation versus the Medium stint. This delivered a P6 finish.

NOR used a two-stop strategy on the 58-lap race: SOFT for laps 1-17 (17 laps, avg 90.41 s, deg -0.029 s/lap), pitting lap 17 to HARD for laps 18-48 (31 laps, avg 88.86 s, deg -0.066 s/lap), then pitting lap 48 to MEDIUM for laps 49-58 (10 laps, avg 86.94 s, deg -0.031 s/lap).  

The longest stint (HARD) produced a lower average lap time than the opening SOFT stint, while the final MEDIUM stint recorded the lowest average. These stint lengths and compounds produced the P7 finish.

ALO ran a one-stop strategy on the 58-lap distance, completing a 36-lap opening stint on HARD (avg. 90.31 s, deg. -0.085 s/lap) before switching to MEDIUM on lap 36 under SC/VSC. The second stint covered the remaining 22 laps (avg. 89.12 s, deg. -0.088 s/lap). This tyre split covered the full race distance and produced the P8 result.

OCO ran a one-stop strategy: 15 laps on soft (avg 90.92 s, deg -0.047 s/lap) followed by a lap-15 pit stop to hard tyres for the final 43 laps (avg 89.29 s, deg -0.025 s/lap). The hard-compound stint delivered both a lower average lap time and roughly half the degradation rate of the soft stint, enabling consistent pace over the remaining race distance and the P9 result.

LEC ran a two-stop strategy on the 58-lap distance. Stint 1 used softs for laps 1-15 (avg 90.58 s, deg -0.004 s/lap), with the first stop on lap 15 switching to hards. Stint 2 used hards for laps 16-35 (avg 89.36 s, deg -0.010 s/lap). The second stop on lap 35 changed to mediums under SC/VSC for the final stint 3 (laps 36-58, avg 89.06 s, deg +0.052 s/lap).  

The hard-to-medium change under SC/VSC enabled the longest stint (23 laps) on the compound with the lowest average lap time in the data, while the earlier soft stint produced the highest average. This sequence produced LEC's P10 finish.

GIO attempted an undercut by pitting on lap 17 (MEDIUM to HARD) while STR extended his MEDIUM stint to lap 21 before pitting (MEDIUM to HARD). This placed GIO on fresh HARD tires for laps 18-21 (part of his 17-lap HARD stint at 89.95 s avg) against STR's older MEDIUM tires (21-lap stint at 91.16 s avg).  

The data shows the GIO-to-STR gap widened from 2.996 s to 4.688 s after these stops. The undercut produced no net gain: GIO's subsequent HARD stint (avg 89.95 s) did not close on STR, and GIO finished P17 behind STR's P13.  

(The later STR stops on laps 21/52 are outside this specific timing window.)

NOR overcut LEC on the first stops by remaining out until lap 17 (SOFT->HARD) while LEC pitted on lap 15 (SOFT->HARD). Per the supplied gap data, this sequence changed the NOR-to-LEC interval from 2.403 s to 2.961 s.  

The later stop therefore produced a net time gain for NOR on the observed interval.  

The overcut succeeded on its immediate metric (gap expansion) and aligned with NOR’s final position (P7) ahead of LEC (P10).

OCO pitted lap 15 (SOFT->HARD) while RIC stayed out until lap 18 (SOFT->HARD). This earlier stop let OCO run fresh HARD tires on laps 16-18, during which RIC continued on 15+ lap SOFTs. The OCO->RIC gap then widened from 1.739s to 3.866s. OCO's subsequent 43-lap HARD stint (avg 89.29s) covered more distance on the compound than RIC's 34-lap HARD stint (avg 89.19s). GIO held VET behind for exactly 12 laps (laps 5-16) despite VET's 0.411 s/lap pace advantage. With teammate_protected=False, the hold produced no teammate-protection benefit and directly delayed VET's forward progress for the full duration.

OCO held RIC behind for exactly 8 laps (38-45) despite RIC's 0.145 s/lap pace advantage. With teammate_protected=True, this hold-up directly delayed RIC and thereby protected OCO's teammate.

LAT held MSC behind for exactly 6 laps (laps 2-7) despite MSC's 0.245 s/lap pace advantage. With teammate_protected=False, the defense was not coordinated to protect any teammate and therefore directly limited MSC's net track position gain over those laps.
ALO held OCO behind for 6 laps (41-46) despite OCO's 0.2 s/lap pace advantage. With teammate_protected=False, the hold did not involve shielding a teammate.
RIC held LEC behind for laps 46-50 despite LEC's 0.008 s/lap pace edge. With teammate_protected=True, the hold protected the teammate.

STR kept VET behind for laps 2-4 despite VET's 0.121 s/lap pace advantage. With teammate_protected=False, the hold produced no teammate-protection benefit and instead directly delayed VET for those three laps.
RUS held GIO behind for laps 22-24 despite GIO's 0.961 s/lap pace advantage. With teammate_protected=True, the hold directly impeded GIO to shield RUS's teammate.
SAI held VER behind for exactly 2 laps (16-17) despite VER's 1.548 s/lap pace advantage. With teammate_protected=False, the hold produced no teammate-protection benefit.

RAI defended by holding position ahead of NOR across laps 18-19, despite NOR's 1.879 s/lap pace advantage. With teammate_protected=False, the hold-up occurred independently of any teammate protection.

PER held HAM behind for exactly 2 laps (19-20) despite HAM's 0.41 s/lap pace advantage, with teammate_protected=True. This defensive hold directly protected the teammate by impeding HAM for those laps.

**Abu Dhabi Grand Prix 2021 (58 laps)**

**Result:** VER won from HAM, SAI, TSU and GAS. VER also set the fastest lap (86.103 s).

**Key strategic moments (data only):**
- OCO undercut RIC, gaining 2.127 s.
- GIO undercut STR, gaining 1.692 s.
- NOR overcut LEC, gaining 0.558 s.
- GIO held VET for 12 laps.
- ALO held GAS for 12 laps.

A final SC/VSC on lap 53 allowed a switch to SOFTs for a 5-lap sprint to the finish. His SOFT pace (avg 86.62s) was marginally faster than his preceding HARD stint.

The strategy was built around using early HARDs for a long, strong stint, then using two SC/VSC interventions to change tyres with minimal time loss—first to a fresh HARD and finally to a SOFT for the finish. This sequence of timely stops, especially the final SOFT switch under SC, secured track position and provided the fastest tyre for the final laps, directly enabling the P1 result.

The degradation rates on both compounds were very similar and extremely low (-0.047s/lap and -0.045s/lap), indicating the HARD tyre was durable over a very long 44-lap stint.

The strategy was a one-stop, committing to a long final stint. The faster and stable pace on the HARD tyre enabled him to compete for the win, but he ultimately finished P2. The data shows no additional stops or compound changes.

The strategy’s outcome was directly shaped by a late Safety Car/VSC period. The SC/VSC interventions enabled two advantageous pit stops without large time loss, allowing a final aggressive stint on fresh SOFT tyres to secure P5.

1.  **Long first stint:** Running the MEDIUM tyre for 30 laps (over half the race) showed low degradation (-0.079s/lap), indicating good tyre management. This allowed him to delay his pit stop, potentially gaining track position.
2.  **Strong second stint:** His average lap time on the HARD tyre (88.17s) was nearly two seconds faster per lap than his MEDIUM stint. Crucially, the HARD tyre showed virtually no degradation (-0.001s/lap), meaning he could push at a consistent, faster pace to the finish without significant tyre wear.
3.  **Pit stop timing:** Pitting on lap 30 placed him on the durable HARD compound for the final 28-lap run to the flag, which provided the pace and consistency needed to secure P6.

The strategy succeeded by maximizing the low-degradation characteristics of both compounds, using the MEDIUM to cover the first half and the very stable HARD to deliver faster, consistent lap times in the second half.

3. **Minimized Total Time**: The long HARD stint avoided a second stop, saving ~20+ seconds in pit lane time versus a two-stop. This was critical for securing points, as OCO’s consistent HARD pace offset any initial time loss from the early stop.

The strategy relied on the HARD’s durability to execute a one-stop, which preserved track position and secured P9 in a race where tire management was key.
